description: Cross-binding pin for `StandardPlacement::placement_score`. Each case carries a `config` (subset of StandardPlacement settings — proximity / anti-affinity axes are skipped because they require runtime closures), a single `candidate` (node_id + tags + metadata), an `artifact` (Daemon with required + optional caps), and an `expected_score`. Bindings — when they ship local StandardPlacement implementations — evaluate and assert the score matches Rust's reference output to within 1e-6 (or `null` for veto). Locks the in-tree axis composition matrix; any regression in scope / intent / colocation / resource / hard-required scoring trips this fixture's assertion. See README.md.

cases.name: default_config_returns_one_for_minimal_candidate
cases.summary: Default StandardPlacement config + empty-cap candidate → all axes identity → score 1.0.

cases.name: hard_required_tag_missing_vetoes
cases.summary: Daemon's required cap set demands `hardware.gpu`; candidate lacks it → hard veto (`null` score).

cases.name: hard_required_tag_satisfied_passes
cases.summary: Same as above but candidate now has `hardware.gpu`. Hard check passes; default config keeps score at 1.0.

cases.name: scope_filter_match_keeps_one
cases.summary: Scope axis with filter `scope:tenant:foo`. Candidate has matching tag → axis returns 1.0 → final 1.0.

cases.name: scope_filter_mismatch_zeroes_score
cases.summary: Scope axis with filter `scope:tenant:foo`. Candidate has a different scope tag (`scope:tenant:bar`) → axis returns 0.0 → composition zeroes the final score (LOCKED §7).

cases.name: scope_filter_no_scope_tags_zeroes_score
cases.summary: Scope axis with non-empty filter; candidate has no `scope:*` tags at all. Axis returns 0.0 → final 0.0. Operators who want unscoped peers to satisfy the axis disable it by leaving `scope_filter` as None.

cases.name: scope_filter_disabled_passes_unscoped_candidate
cases.summary: Scope axis disabled (config omits `scope_filter`). Candidate has no scope tags. Axis returns 1.0 (identity) → final 1.0. Pin the back-compat default for daemons that don't care about scoping.

cases.name: resource_axis_no_data_is_permissive
cases.summary: Resource axis under default `Compute` config. Candidate has no resource tags. Axis returns 1.0 — `can't measure, default permissive` semantics. Operators wanting strict resource enforcement use the daemon's required caps + the hard-constraint check.
